text: The person operating the DaVinci XI robot manually rotates the boom to align the laser line parallel with the trajectory of the ports. Behind arms one and four, a clutch button is pressed to rotate the upper portion of the boom. The boom's height is adjusted by pressing the port clutch button for three seconds, allowing the vertical arm to move up or down. Once aligned, all arms are docked to the robot.

The surgeon aligns the trajectory of the camera port with the center of the dissection, arranging the flex joints parallel to the camera port arm. The laser at the top of the robot is pointed at the camera port, followed by the flex joints. The patient clearance joints are adjusted to descend as far as possible without contacting the patient.
